Tests the Math.atan2 function with Infinity arguments to verify it returns Math.PI / 4.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of the `Math.atan2` function in JavaScript. It verifies that when both arguments to `Math.atan2` are `Infinity`, the function returns `Math.PI / 4`, which is the expected angle in radians. This test ensures that the `Math.atan2` function correctly handles cases where both inputs are positive infinity, which is a specific edge case in mathematical computations. The code is a single line script that serves as a unit test for this particular mathematical function.
